task_description: Find a men's bomber jacket, review its product media, and proceed to buy it immediately.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped view shows a semitransparent red X placed on an empty white area directly below the multicolored Google logo, not on any button, icon, text field border, or suggestion item. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open on the Google homepage. The central search panel is active with a blinking text cursor inside it, and a dropdown of search/history suggestions is visible below, including entries such as "amazon - Google 搜索", "armason", "Uber Eats", "GitHub", "spotify", "google calendar", "booking", and "google map". The click target is a blank region inside the white search suggestion panel.
caption.think: The user is clicking a blank area of the open Google search suggestions panel, likely to place focus there or dismiss/confirm the current search box state without selecting any specific suggestion.
caption.action: Click the blank white area inside the open search suggestions panel beneath the Google logo.
caption.expectation: The search box should remain focused or the suggestions panel may dismiss, with no specific suggestion selected and no navigation occurring immediately.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped view centers on the Google homepage search field, which is active and contains the text "Amazon" with the text cursor at the end, indicating typing into the main search box rather than selecting a suggestion. Directly below it, the autocomplete panel is open and shows suggestions including "amazon - Google 搜索", an Amazon result with the Amazon logo and Chinese subtitle, and other entries such as "amazon 香港", "amazon 深圳", "amazon us", "amazon prime", "amazon shopping", and "amazon mexico". In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open to a new tab on google.com in Chinese, with the large multicolored Google logo above the centered search box and the suggestions dropdown expanded beneath it.
caption.think: The user is entering the search term AMAZON into Google's main search field, likely to search for Amazon. The typed text appears to be replacing the previously empty query area rather than choosing one of the suggestions.
caption.action: Type "AMAZON" into the Google search box.
caption.expectation: The search field should display AMAZON, and the autocomplete suggestions should update to match that query while focus remains in the search box.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped view shows the Google homepage search area with the query text "Amazon" already entered in the rounded search box. A semitransparent red X is visible over the middle of the autocomplete dropdown panel, but the raw action is a keyboard action rather than a click. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open on Google in Chinese, with the multicolored Google logo centered above the search field and a suggestion list expanded below it. The top suggestion shows the Amazon logo and the text "Amazon" with the Chinese subtitle "亚马逊—公司", followed by entries such as "Amazon - Google 搜索", "amazon 香港", "amazon 深圳", "amazon us", and others.
caption.think: The user has finished entering the search term and wants to submit the search from the active Google search box using the keyboard, not select a specific suggestion with the mouse.
caption.action: Press ENTER to submit the Google search for "Amazon".
caption.expectation: Google should load the search results page for Amazon, replacing the autocomplete dropdown with search results.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped view shows a Google search result card for Amazon.com. A semitransparent red X is centered directly on the blue hyperlink text reading "Amazon.com Official Site | Shop Online Today with Amazon," which is the main clickable result title beneath the small Amazon icon, the label "Amazon.com," and the URL "https://www.amazon.com." In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is displaying Google search results for "amazon" in Chinese. The Amazon sponsored result appears in the left results column, and an Amazon information panel with images and a blue website button is visible on the right.
caption.think: The user wants to open the main Amazon website from the Google results by selecting the primary Amazon.com search result link.
caption.action: Click the blue result title link "Amazon.com Official Site | Shop Online Today with Amazon."
caption.expectation: The Amazon website should open, likely navigating the current Chrome tab from the Google results page to Amazon.com.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped view shows the long Amazon search field in the top header. A semitransparent red X is centered on the empty white interior of the search box, beneath the placeholder text "Search Amazon," indicating the target is the search input area itself rather than a button or menu. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open to the Amazon homepage. The Amazon header is visible with the logo on the left, a category dropdown labeled "All," the search bar across the top, account and orders links on the right, and the main homepage content below with promotional tiles. A small Google Translate language popup is also visible near the upper-right area, but the X is not on it.
caption.think: The user likely wants to place the text cursor into Amazon's main search field so they can begin entering a product search. This is a top-area UI control, and clicking it should activate the input field.
caption.expectation: The search field should gain focus and show a text cursor, entering text-edit mode so the user can type a search query.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped view shows Amazon’s main search field in the top header with the category dropdown set to "All." The text currently visible in the search box is "jacket," indicating the search field is active and text entry is happening in that field rather than on a button. A white autocomplete panel is expanded underneath with suggestions such as "jackets for women," "jackets," "jackets for women trendy," "jackets for women casual," "jacket hanger," and "jacket rack." In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open on the Amazon homepage. The Amazon logo is at the top left, the magnifying-glass search button is at the right end of the search bar, and the page below is dimmed behind the dropdown. A shipping notice popup near the upper left says items are being shown for Hong Kong and includes "Dismiss" and "Change Address" buttons.
caption.think: The user is continuing to enter a product query into Amazon’s search bar. Based on the raw action, they intend to type JACKETS into the already focused search field, refining or extending the current text toward a jackets search rather than clicking a suggestion yet.
caption.action: Type "JACKETS" into the Amazon search box, appending to the existing text in the active field.
caption.expectation: The search text in the Amazon search bar should update to include the typed word, and the autocomplete suggestions below should refresh to match the new jackets-related query.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped view shows the top portion of the Amazon page with the search area active; a semitransparent red X is visible over the page, but the raw action is a keyboard action. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open on Amazon.com. The Amazon search bar at the top contains the text "jackets," and a white autocomplete dropdown is expanded below with suggestions including "jackets for women," "jackets," "jackets for women trendy," "jackets for women casual," "jacket hanger," and "jacket rack." A shipping popup about showing items that ship to Hong Kong is also visible, but the focused task is clearly the active search field.
caption.think: The user has already entered a search term and now wants to submit it rather than click a suggestion. Pressing Enter should run the Amazon search for the typed query.
caption.action: Press Enter to submit the search for "jackets."
caption.expectation: Amazon should load the search results page for jackets, and the autocomplete suggestions should disappear.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped view shows a product image of a black bomber-style jacket with a semitransparent red X placed on the left sleeve/chest area of the jacket photo. This is an object on the page, not a toolbar or blank area. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is displaying Amazon search results for "jackets." The clicked item is the third product card in the results row, labeled with a small orange "Best Seller" tag and titled "TACVASEN Men's Bomber Jacket Lightweight Casual Spring Fall Windbreaker Zip Up Coat with Pocket."
caption.think: The user likely wants to open this specific jacket's product page from the Amazon search results by clicking its product image.
caption.action: Click the black jacket product image for "TACVASEN Men's Bomber Jacket Lightweight Casual Spring Fall Windbreaker Zip Up Coat with Pocket."
caption.expectation: The selected product should open, typically loading the Amazon product detail page for that jacket.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped view shows the Amazon product page itself without any semitransparent red X, consistent with a scroll action rather than a click. Visible content includes a large black bomber jacket product image on the left, the product title "TACVASEN Men's Bomber Jacket Lightweight Casual Spring Fall Windbreaker Zip Up Coat with Pocket" in the center, color swatches and size buttons below, and the purchase panel on the right with price, quantity dropdown, and yellow "Add to cart" plus orange "Buy Now" buttons. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is displaying this Amazon product detail page, and the page appears positioned around the middle-upper product details area. A small shipping popup about showing items that ship to Hong Kong is still open near the upper left. The action is a scroll up on the main webpage, so the page content is intended to move upward toward earlier content near the top of the product listing.
caption.think: The user is likely adjusting the Amazon product page upward to revisit content nearer the top of the product page, such as the main image area, title, ratings, or header details, rather than interacting with any specific control.
caption.action: Scroll up on the main Amazon product page.
caption.expectation: The webpage should move upward, revealing slightly earlier content higher on the product page while the product page remains open in Chrome.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a vertical strip of small product thumbnails on the Amazon product page. A semitransparent red X is centered on the bottom thumbnail, which has a blue selection border and appears to show a lifestyle image with a person wearing the jacket. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open on the Amazon product page for "TACVASEN Men's Bomber Jacket Lightweight Casual Spring Fall Windbreaker Zip Up Coat with Pocket." The left side contains the main product media area and thumbnail column, the center shows the title, ratings, price, and color options, and the right side shows the purchase panel. A shipping popup for Hong Kong is still visible near the upper left. The click target is an object on the page: a product image thumbnail in the media gallery.
caption.think: The user likely wants to switch the main product display to this specific thumbnail image to inspect a different product photo or media item.
caption.action: Click the bottom image thumbnail in the left-side product gallery, the one showing a person wearing the jacket and outlined in blue.
caption.expectation: The main product media area should update to show the selected image, and the thumbnail should remain or become the active selected item.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows the Amazon main product media area with a lifestyle image: a person wearing a maroon bomber jacket standing outdoors, overlaid with large black-and-white text reading "BOMBER JACKET." A semitransparent red X is centered on the circular play button in the middle of this image, indicating the target is the video play control on the product media object. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open to the Amazon product page for "TACVASEN Men's Bomber Jacket Lightweight Casual Spring Fall Windbreaker Zip Up Coat with Pocket." A vertical strip of thumbnails appears to the left, the product title and color options are in the center, the purchase panel is on the right, and a shipping popup for Hong Kong remains visible near the upper left. This is an object on the page, not a browser toolbar control or blank region.
caption.think: The user likely wants to play the product video from the main media gallery to inspect how the jacket looks when worn. Clicking the centered play button should activate the selected media rather than changing browser state.
caption.action: Click the circular play button centered on the main product image/video preview showing the model in the bomber jacket.
caption.expectation: The product video should start playing or open in Amazon's media viewer, with the selected media entering playback mode.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped view shows the full Amazon product page with no semitransparent red X, consistent with a scroll action. Visible content includes the product title "TACVASEN Men's Bomber Jacket Lightweight Casual Spring Fall Windbreaker Zip Up Coat with Pocket," a large mostly blank main media area on the left with a vertical strip of jacket thumbnails, color swatches for multiple jacket colors in the center, size options near the bottom with "Large" selected, and the purchase panel on the right with price, quantity, yellow "Add to cart," and orange "Buy Now" buttons. A shipping popup about items shipping to Hong Kong remains open near the upper left. The full-screen context confirms this is the main webpage in Google Chrome, and the scrollable region is the product page itself rather than a separate panel or media viewer.
caption.think: The user likely wants to continue moving down the Amazon product page after checking the media, to see lower sections such as additional purchase options, product details, descriptions, or reviews.
caption.action: Scroll down on the main Amazon product page.
caption.expectation: The page should move downward, bringing content below the current product options and purchase area further into view while remaining on the same Amazon listing.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped view shows the right-side Amazon purchase panel. A semitransparent red X is centered on the orange rounded rectangular button labeled "Buy Now," directly below the yellow "Add to cart" button and beneath the quantity dropdown. In the full-screen context, this is the Amazon product page in Google Chrome for the TACVASEN men's bomber jacket, with product images and details in the center, the purchase box on the right, and a shipping popup still visible near the upper left. The target is an object on the page, specifically a purchase button rather than a browser control or blank area.
caption.think: The user likely wants to proceed directly to Amazon's immediate purchase flow for this jacket instead of adding it to the cart first.
caption.action: Click the orange "Buy Now" button in the right-side purchase panel.
caption.expectation: Amazon should open the next purchase step, typically taking the user to sign-in or checkout flow for immediate purchase.
